payload.title: Rustfmt support: nightly opt-in or alternative formatter
payload.description: rustfmt parses with rustc, so it rejects the dialect's syntax extensions (named args, pipe, effect keyword). On stable, the 'ignore' config in rustfmt.toml is unsupported (warns + does nothing). Workaround landed in CI: explicit -p list of every non-strict crate, auto-derived via cargo metadata. Real fix options: (a) require nightly rustfmt for strict crates (rustfmt.toml ignore works there); (b) add a 'rustricted fmt' subcommand that lowers + invokes rustfmt + un-lowers; (c) restrict the workspace to one rustfmt config and keep strict files out of cargo fmt --all entirely. Surfaced by RT-6.
